mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases in Kubernetes environments, providing a seamless operational lifecycle from deployment to maintenance. It is ideal for teams looking to integrate PostgreSQL into their Kubernetes workflows with a focus on automation and GitOps practices. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native features for high availability.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

```

> This repository contains the CloudNativePG Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This command installs the CloudNativePG operator in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

```

> Create a namespace for the operator.
> Install the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a PostgreSQL cluster with 3 instances.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Check the status of the PostgreSQL cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL cluster and all its data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstall the operator.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backup the current cluster configuration.
> Upgrade the operator to the specified version.
> Verify the cluster status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes API server is unreachable or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the Kubernetes API server status and ensure there are enough resources available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the PostgreSQL pods are not starting due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Storage class not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified storage class in the cluster configuration does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the storage class is created and available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cannot connect to PostgreSQL'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the PostgreSQL service is not exposed correctly or if network policies are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and network policies to ensure connectivity.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore functionalities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.28.0, ensure that your storage classes are updated to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg', showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
